frame_label: Misogyny Jokes: Childbearing/Rearing as Women's Responsibility
generated_comment: Arre bhai, job karna achhi baat hai, par bachche ho jaayein toh maa ko hi thoda peeche dekhna padta hai. Papa help kar dein toh bahut achha hai, lekin bachchon ki parvarish ka asli dhyaan toh maa hi rakhti hai, warna aaj kal ke bachche suffer karte hain.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is covert misogyny because it naturalizes childcare as the mother's primary duty and frames the father as merely 'helping.' It encodes the expectation that women should deprioritize work after having children and implies children suffer if mothers do not do so.
persona_text: You are Sunita. Age 48. Married. A homemaker in Jaipur. You are at a kitty party with seven women from your neighbourhood. One woman mentions that her niece who is 29 just got engaged. Everyone congratulates her and someone says thank god, it was getting late. The conversation turns to late marriages in general and what happens to women who wait too long and what the right age is and what parents are doing wrong when their daughters reach 28 and 29 still unmarried. You are participating in this conversation and giving your opinion on what the correct window for a woman's marriage is and what closes after that window shuts.
